selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":58,"gender":"woman","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"cleaner","city_of_living":"Chicago","employment_status":"employed full-time","education_level":"upper secondary","parents_education":{"parent_1":"bachelor's degree","parent_2":"primary school"},"current_marital_status":"in a relationship","children":2,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"curious","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend a few times monthly on social media","hobbies":["knitting","reading novels","painting","birdwatching","cinema"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":42,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":68,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":84,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":75,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":29,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to have a critical eye when scrolling through your feeds. Here are some tips to help you identify and avoid fake content: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Check the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 3) Look for corroboration from other sources before jumping to conclusions. 4) Use fact-checking websites and tools to verify the accuracy of the content. By following these steps, you can protect yourself from fake news and maintain a healthy social media experience."
,"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a critical eye when scrolling through your feeds. By following these steps, you can protect yourself from fake news and maintain a healthy social media experience."

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to have a critical eye when scrolling through your feeds. Here are some tips to help you identify and avoid fake content: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Check the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 3) Look for corroboration from other sources before jumping to conclusions. 4) Use fact-checking websites and tools to verify the accuracy of the content. By following these steps, you can protect yourself from fake news and maintain a healthy social media experience.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a critical eye when scrolling through your feeds. Here are some tips to help you identify and avoid fake content: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Check the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 3) Look for corroboration from other sources before jumping to conclusions. 4) Use fact-checking websites and tools to verify the accuracy of the content. By following these steps, you can protect yourself from fake news and maintain a healthy social media experience.
